summary: Matched the Hammer item implementation by replacing asm/melee/it/items/ithammer.s with src/melee/it/items/ithammer.c and adding ithammer.h. The PR body says it rebased a previous draft, corrected struct-usage typing issues, and matched data. The C version reconstructs the Hammer item state table, special attributes struct, state callbacks, animation/state transitions, gravity/fall behavior, random special-attribute selection, and owner effect vector logic while updating obj_files.mk to build the C object instead of the asm object.
what_changed: Deleted the 507-line Hammer item assembly file asm/melee/it/items/ithammer.s.
Added a 215-line C implementation in src/melee/it/items/ithammer.c for functions in the it_80293D94 through it_80294344 range.
Added src/melee/it/items/ithammer.h with include guard MELEE_IT_ITEMS_ITHAMMER_H, an itHammerData struct, the extern ItemStateTable it_803F6150 declaration, and Hammer function prototypes.
Changed obj_files.mk so the build uses $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/it/items/ithammer.c.o instead of the corresponding asm object.
Reconstructed ItemStateTable it_803F6150 with six state entries using callbacks such as it_80293FF4, it_80294080, it_80294170, it_8029424C, it_8029427C, it_80294304, it_8029430C, and it_80294310.
Introduced itHammerData with fields x0, x4, and x8, used by it_80293DCC to select a random value with HSD_Randf/HSD_Randi based on x8 / 100.0f.

Rebuilt repeated Hammer item behavior around Item_80268E5C state changes, xD4C countdown/state flag checks, it_80272CC0/it_80272A3C joint or model cleanup, and item physics callbacks like it_80272860.
Implemented owner/effect vector logic in it_80293E34 using lb_8000B1CC, lbVector_Normalize, sign/absolute-value handling of result.x, and it_80299C48.
smart_moves: Moved the file from asm to C and updated obj_files.mk in the same PR, making the matching status explicit and preventing stale asm from remaining in the build.

Added a dedicated itHammerData struct for the Hammer special attributes rather than leaving the article data as raw offsets; this directly addresses the PR body's note about correcting typing issues from struct usages.
Factored the repeated xD4C <= 0 cleanup pattern into a small static inline helper named thing, reducing duplicated calls to it_80272CC0(..., 3) followed by it_80272A3C.

Used existing item system types and helpers, including Item_GObj, Item, Article, ItemAttr, ItemStateTable, GET_ITEM, and ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE, which makes the translation align with nearby item decomp conventions.
Preserved address-based symbol names for still-unnamed functions, avoiding speculative semantic naming while still making the code buildable and matchable.
Kept the Hammer item state table data in C, which was important because the PR body explicitly says it also matches data.
decomp_lessons: For item decompilation, matching often requires both code and item data: state tables like ItemStateTable it_803F6150 need to move alongside the functions that reference them.

When article special attributes are accessed through xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes, introducing a small item-specific struct can resolve typing issues and make random/parameter logic clearer.
Repeated item lifecycle checks around Item.xD4C can often be expressed as a helper, but the helper name should remain conservative if semantics are not known.

Small expression choices can matter for matching: it_80293E34 uses ternaries for sign selection and absolute-value behavior on result.x, reflecting the assembly's branch/floating-point conversion pattern.
Formatting of nested state-table arrays can create noisy diffs or reviewer concern; trailing commas inside inner arrays may interact poorly with clang-format in this codebase.
Use established item callback signatures and return types when reconstructing state callbacks: several callbacks return bool/int zero, while others are void animation or physics callbacks.
naming_conventions: File names use the item prefix style: ithammer.c and ithammer.h under src/melee/it/items/.
Unidentified functions retain address-style names such as it_80293D94, it_8029402C, and it_80294310.
Item-specific special attributes are named with an item-prefixed struct, itHammerData.
The header guard follows the path-derived all-caps convention MELEE_IT_ITEMS_ITHAMMER_H.
Global item state table symbols retain address-style data names, here ItemStateTable it_803F6150[].
Struct fields remain offset-like names x0, x4, and x8 when semantic names are not evidenced.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted a full item asm file to C and removed the asm object from the build list.
Matched random range logic from assembly with HSD_Randf() < temp_r31->x8 / 100.0f and HSD_Randi(temp_r31->x4 - temp_r31->x0) + temp_r31->x0.
Matched bitfield checks from Item.xDC8_word.flags for entered-hitlag behavior in it_80293D94.
Used Vec3 stack temporaries, lb_8000B1CC joint position reads, lbVector_Normalize, and ternaries for sign/absolute value to represent the PowerPC floating-point/vector sequence in it_80293E34.
Used Item_80268E5C with numeric animation/state IDs and ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE to mirror repeated state transitions.
Used a static inline helper for repeated branch tail code, likely helping code generation while removing manual duplication.
Preserved external declarations for not-yet-declared item helpers such as it_8026D62C, it_8026DF34, it_8026E15C, it_8026E8C4, it_80272CC0, and it_80299C48.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested removing commas at the end of each inner array in ItemStateTable initialization to make formatting less crazy.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Explained that the trailing-comma style followed precedent already set in other item files, though those files were not formatted.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename Hammer functions or replace address-style symbols in src/melee/it/items/ithammer.c.
Search for follow-up cleanup of the helper named thing in ithammer.c.
Check whether itHammerData fields x0, x4, and x8 were later given semantic names.
Look for later header cleanup moving extern declarations for it_8026D62C, it_8026DF34, it_8026E15C, it_8026E8C4, it_80272CC0, or it_80299C48 into shared headers.
Compare formatting of ItemStateTable initializers in other item files after the trailing-comma review comment.
